data.title: Iren doubles NVIDIA GPU fleet
data.description:
Iren (NASDAQ:IREN) said on Monday that it has doubled its NVIDIA GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) fleet, and has secured financing for a prior purchase of NVIDIA Blackwell B200 and B300 GPUs.

The bitcoin mining data centers operator has procured an additional 4.2K NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs for ~$193M, bringing its total GPU fleet to ~8.5K NVIDIA GPUs.

Total installed mining capacity is expected to remain at ~50 EH/s.

Additionally, the company announced a $102M financing, structured as a 36-month lease for 100% of the purchase price of the GPUs.

The lease payments are calculated using a high single-digit interest rate.

"Our expanded Blackwell capacity positions IREN to capture strong demand and drive the next phase of our AI Cloud revenue growth," said Co-CEO Daniel Roberts.

Shares were -1.17% pre-market to $21.18.

data.title: Driven Brands names Mo Khalid as COO
data.description:
• Driven Brands (NASDAQ:DRVN) has appointed Mo Khalid as executive vice president and chief operating officer.
  • Khalid currently serves as executive vice president and president of Take 5. He re-joined Driven Brands in 2023 as group president of the maintenance segment, which at the time included Take 5 Oil Change and Meineke.
  • Tim Austin, the current chief operating officer of Take 5, will take over as president, succeeding Khalid.

data.title: U.S. reportedly confirms first human screwworm case
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) said on Sunday it has detected the first human case in the country of New World screwworm, a flesh-eating parasite, linked to travel from an outbreak-affected nation.

The case, confirmed by the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention on Aug. 4, involved a patient who had recently returned from El Salvador, HHS spokesperson Andrew G. Nixon told Reuters in an email.

The investigation was conducted in coordination with the Maryland Department of Health. Nixon noted that "The risk to public health in the United States from this introduction is very low.”

Screwworms can be devastating in cattle and wildlife, and, though human cases are rare, infestations in either animals or people can be fatal. Treatment is arduous, requiring the removal of hundreds of larvae and thorough wound disinfection. However, infestations are generally survivable if treated promptly.

The government’s confirmation comes just over a week after U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins traveled to Texas to announce plans for a new sterile-fly production facility to help combat the pest.

The USDA estimates that a screwworm outbreak could cost Texas—the nation’s largest cattle-producing state—about $1.8 billion in livestock losses, labor costs, and medication expenses.

The presence of screwworms can likely rattle beef and livestock markets. Adding to industry anxiety, an executive at the Beef Alliance emailed about two dozen individuals in the livestock and beef sectors last week, stating that the CDC had confirmed a human screwworm case in Maryland in a traveler from Guatemala, a source told Reuters.

Conflicting reports from industry sources and the U.S. government about the human case are likely to deepen uncertainty in a sector already on edge, as livestock traders and beef producers worry about potential cattle infections amid record-high prices and the smallest U.S. herd in nearly seven decades.

Screwworms have been advancing north through Mexico from Central America since 2023 and remain endemic in Cuba, Haiti, the Dominican Republic and several South American nations, according to the USDA.

Mexico reported a fresh case in July in Ixhuatlán de Madero, Veracruz—about 370 miles (595 km) south of the U.S. border—prompting the USDA to immediately shut livestock trade through southern ports of entry. The agency had previously halted imports in November and May as part of efforts to prevent the pest from entering the U.S.

Retailers with imported beef in supply chains: Walmart (WMT), Kroger (KR), Albertsons Companies (ACI) and Costco Wholesale (COST).

data.title: Avino acquires full ownership of La Preciosa silver project in Mexico in $22M deal
data.description:
Avino Silver & Gold Mines (NYSE:ASM) said Monday it acquired 100% ownership of its La Preciosa project in Mexico from Deterra Royalties for $22M, including a $13.25M upfront payment followed by an $8.75M payment deferred for one year.

La Preciosa is one of the largest undeveloped silver resources in Mexico, and is located adjacent to Avino's (NYSE:ASM) existing mine and infrastructure, the company said.

Avino (ASM) said it has made excellent progress advancing La Preciosa, with first production expected by year-end 2025 and significant production growth expected in the following years which will see La Preciosa become a cornerstone asset for the company.

"By eliminating the royalty burden immediately prior to commencing production, we believe we will generate meaningful returns on our investment by lowering La Preciosa's operating cost profile, and ensuring that as much of La Preciosa's value remains with the operator and its stakeholders," President and CEO David Wolfin said.

data.title: Dongfeng Motors soars in Hong Kong trading after go-private, relisting announcement
data.description:
Chinese state automaker Dongfeng Motor Group (OTCPK:DNFGF) (OTCPK:DNFGY) surged 54% in Hong Kong trading on Monday after the company announced plans to essentially switch its listing to the group's pure electric vehicle arm, Voyah.

Late on Friday, Dongfeng (OTCPK:DNFGF) (OTCPK:DNFGY) said it would delist from the Hong Kong Exchange after being absorbed by state-owned parent Dongfeng Motor Corp., while Voyah shares would be distributed to existing shareholders and the unit would join the Hong Kong exchange bourse by way of introduction, which is a method for securities already in issue.

The company set a one-time cash offer of HK$6.68 per Dongfeng Motor Group share (OTCPK:DNFGF) DNFGY), along with 0.3552608 Voyah shares each. Based on the midpoint of Voyah's estimated range. The aggregate value of the offer adds up to HK$10.85 per share, or an 81.7% premium on the pre-suspension closing price.

Citi raised its price target on Dongfeng's (OTCPK:DNFGF) (OTCPK:DNFGY) to HK$10.34 from HK$6.20 following the announcement. J.P. Morgan analyst Nick Lai said the unexpected Dongfeng's (OTCPK:DNFGF) (OTCPK:DNFGY) announcement is a clear indication of where the Chinese government and Dongfeng (OTCPK:DNFGF) (OTCPK:DNFGY) management aim to unlock the hidden value of the company.

data.title: Axogen falls 16% after FDA delays Avance Nerve Graft decision to December
data.description:
Axogen (NASDAQ:AXGN) shares fell 16% in premarket trading after the company announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration had extended the Prescription Drug User Fee Act goal date for its biologics license application for Avance Nerve Graft by three months.

On August 22, the company received a communication from the FDA indicating that the information submitted by the company in response to an FDA information request, which included substantial new manufacturing and facility data not previously reviewed by the agency, was deemed a major amendment to its application. 

Under FDA guidelines, this designation allows for additional time to review the submission. As a result, the FDA has extended the PDUFA goal date to December 5, 2025.

The FDA also informed Axogen (NASDAQ:AXGN) that it now anticipates providing feedback on product labeling in November 2025, consistent with PDUFA review procedures.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: PDD Holdings beats Q2 earnings and revenue estimates
NAPCO Security GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.06, revenue of $50.7M beats by $6.25M
data.description:
• NAPCO Security press release (NASDAQ:NSSC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $50.7M (+0.7% Y/Y) beats by $6.25M.
  • Recurring service revenue ("RSR") increased 10% and 14% to $22.4 million and $86.3 million for Q4 and the full year respectively, with a 91% gross margin for both periods

data.title: Timken names Honeywell exec Lucian Boldea as CEO
data.description:
The Timken Company (NYSE:TKR) on Monday said that its Board of Directors has appointed Lucian Boldea the company's president and CEO and member of the Board, effective Sept. 1.

Boldea will succeed Richard G. Kyle, who has been in an interim role since March after having previously served as president and CEO from 2014 to 2024.

Boldea joins Timken (NYSE:TKR) from Honeywell (HON), where he served as President and CEO of Industrial Automation.

data.title: Abercrombie & Fitch announces a fashion partnership with the NFL
data.description:
Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) announced on Monday a multi-year partnership with The National Football League that makes the retailer the league's first Official NFL Fashion Partner. Men’s and women’s apparel, including hoodies, sweatshirts, t-shirts, outerwear, and accessories representing all 32 NFL teams, is now available in select Abercrombie stores and online.

Building on the success since launching licensed NFL products in 2022, Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) said its aim is to redefine fan style through a fashion-first lens in partnership with the NFL. To celebrate the partnership, Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) debuted a national seasonal campaign "Style Concierge" featuring players known for their personal style off the field, including Christian McCaffrey (San Francisco 49ers), Amon-Ra St. Brown (Detroit Lions), CeeDee Lamb (Dallas Cowboys), and Tee Higgins (Cincinnati Bengals).

Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) CEO Fran Horowitz said the company is uniquely positioned for this moment where sports, fashion, and culture are converging like never before.

"By pairing athlete-led campaigns and player-designed apparel with a lifestyle assortment, Abercrombie will help blur the line between outfits worn during game day arrivals and everyday wear for both female and male fans as well as for players. In addition, Abercrombie will activate across NFL tentpole events, including select NFL international games and within its retail locations worldwide," highlighted the company.

Shares of Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) were up 0.6% in premarket trading. The mall stock is down 34.1% on a year-to-date basis.
